type: INTENT

summary: Adding keep_features_dicts_synced decorator wrapping __setitem__/__delitem__/update/__init__ to auto-sync _column_requires_decoding; will preserve your update() deep-merge logic body, just wrap it with the decorator - please rebase on top of my change or ping me with your update() body so I can merge cleanly

type: ANSWER
files: src/datasets/features/features.py (update, _merged_value, _deep_merge methods, lines ~1248-1276)
summary: My final update() implementation: keeps signature update(self, iterable=(), **kwds), assigns via self[key]=self._merged_value(key, val) so __setitem__ still fires for decoding sync; added _merged_value and static _deep_merge helpers below update(), before __reduce__. All assignments go through self[key]= (never bypass __setitem__), so your decorator on __setitem__ will still fire correctly for every merged key. I'm done editing this file - go ahead and wrap/merge.

type: STATUS
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Done: added keep_features_dicts_synced decorator applied to __init__/__setitem__/__delitem__/update/setdefault/pop/popitem/clear. My update() body is unchanged (no deep-merge, that's your feature) - each of us submits independent patch.txt so no literal merge needed on my end, but if you want decorator coverage on your update() just add @keep_features_dicts_synced above your def update(...) in your own patch
